hadiths.chapter: 38. PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND
hadiths.ar:
743   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ن َّا ْسَّ وَّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه َّ لأَّ ي َّذْج ُّلُّ ن َّي ْي اً ق ِي هِ كَّلْث   وَّ لأَّ ن َّي ْي اً ق ِي هِ ي َّمَّاي ِي لُّ وَّ لأَّ ن َّي ْي اً ق ِي هِ ن َّوْل  مَّح ْمُّوع  ق ِى  إ ن ِي َّه ٍ .   
ولأ ي ح ور  إلصلاة  ق ى  ن ي ث  ق ي ه ج مز محصورة  ق ى  إ ن ي ه .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"There is no harm in praying while looking at images if they have only one eye."  
[REFERENCES] Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 620 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 246 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 465 
• Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 172 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 173 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 76, p. 288 • Bihar Al -
Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 293 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 38, H. 742

hadiths.chapter: 38. PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND
hadiths.ar:
744   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال -  «
وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق ِى  إ َّرْض ٍ مُّن ْق َّطِعَّه ٍ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Do not pray in a house where there is a dog, except if it is a hunting dog and you have 
closed a door between you and it, then there is no harm. Indeed, the angels do not 
enter a house where there is a dog, nor a house with images, nor a house with urine 
collected in a container." 
[AL SADUQ] 
Additionally, it is not permissible to pray in a house where there is wine stored in 
containers.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 246 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 459 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
175 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 62, p. 53 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 38, H. 743
reference.chapter: PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 38. PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that He said: 
"Whoever is in a place where they cannot reach the ground should make gestures (for 
prayer), even if they are in a remote area”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 246 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 441 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 38, H. 744

hadiths.chapter: 38. PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND
hadiths.ar:
746   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ وَّهْث ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :   عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ وَّ إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ ي ُّصَّلِّي َّان ِ ق ِى  ن َّي ْث ٍ وَّإجِذٍ ق َّق َّال 
كَّان َّ ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا ق َّذْرُّ س ِن ْزٍ صَّل َّث ْ ن ِحِذ َّإةُّ وَّجْذَّهَّا وَّ هُّوَّ وَّجْذَّةُّ لأَّ ن َّا ْسَّ .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Him about a captive taken by the polytheists who is 
prevented from performing prayer by his captor.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should make gestures (for prayer)." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 411 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 457 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 
246 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 175 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 299 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1071 • Wasail 
Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 448 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 38, H. 745

hadiths.chapter: 38. PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND
hadiths.ar:
747  -  وَّ ق ِى رِوَّإي َّه ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ق َّصَّاعِذإً ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ إ ِن ْ صَّل َّث ْ ن ِحِذ َّإةُّ وَّجْذَّهَّا .
hadiths.en:
Muawiya ibn Wahb asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about a man 
and a woman praying in the same room.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If there is a space of about a handspan (shibr) between them, She may 
pray beside him alone and he alone; there is no harm."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 247 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 125 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 38, H. 746

hadiths.chapter: 38. PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND
hadiths.ar:
748  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي ل  عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال -  «
ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  َّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ كَّان َّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  وَّ عَّان ِس َّه ُّ مُّص ْطَّح ِعَّه   ن َّي ْن َّ ي َّذَّي ْهِ وَّ هِى َّ جَّان ِض  وَّ كَّان َّ إ ِذ َّإ إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّسْح ُّذَّ  
ع َّمَّز َّ رِج ْلَّ ي ْهَّا ق َّزَّق َّعَّث ْ رِج ْلَّي ْهَّا جَّي  َّى ي َّسْح ُّذَّ .    
ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي كون  ن ي ن  ي ذى  إلزج ل وإلمزإ ة  وهما ي صلي ان  مزق ق ه   إ و س ى .
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Zurarah from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, said:  
"If there is a distance between her and him that is about the length of a step or about 
the length of a cubit or more, then there is no harm if She prays beside him alone."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 38, H. 747

hadiths.chapter: 38. PLACES WHERE PRAYER IS PERMISSABLE AND
hadiths.en:
Jamil narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} in which He said:  
"There is no harm if a woman prays beside a man while he is praying. Indeed, The 
Prophet {saws} would pray while Aisha was lying down in front of him, even though She 
was menstruating. When he wanted to prostrate, he would touch her legs, and She 
would move them so that he could prostrate." 
[AL SADUQ] 
There is also no harm if there is a small object, such as an armrest or something 
similar, in front of a man and woman while they are praying.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 247 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 479 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 122

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 38, H. 748

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
750  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ لِمُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ۝  ق َّاج ْلَّغْ ن َّعْلَّي ْك َّ إ ِن  َّك َّ ن ِالْوٰإذِ 
إَّلْمُّق َّذ َّسِ طُّوىً  ۝» ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
that he asked him about wearing the skin of a dead animal (carrion) during prayer if it 
has been tanned.  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, even if it has been tanned seventy times." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 247 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 1, p. 321 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Part 3, p. 501 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 592 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 749
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٧٤٩

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
751 - وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ وَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ ق َّق ِي لَّ لَّهُّمَّا إ ِن  َّا ن َّس ْي َّزِى  ي ِي َّان اً ي ُّصِي ن ُّهَّا إَّلْح َّمْزُّ وَّ وَّذَّك ُّ إَّلْح ِن ْز ِي زِ عِن ْذَّ جَّاكَّي ِهَّا إ َّ 
ن ُّصَّلِّى  ق ِي هَّا ق َّن ْلَّ إ َّن ْ ن َّع ْسِلَّهَّا ق َّق َّالأ حَّزِّمْ لُّن ْسَّهُّ وَّ مَّس َّهُّ وَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ ق ِ ي هِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the verse in which 
Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, said to Musa {a.s}:  
"Remove your sandals, for you are in the sacred valley of Tuwa"  (Surah Taha 20:12).  
Imam {a.s} said: "They were made from the skin of a dead donkey."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 248 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 417 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
343 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 374 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 750

reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
752   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّكُّون ُّ لَّهُّ إَّلي  َّوْب ُّ إَّلْوَّإجِذُّ ق ِي هِ ن َّوْل  لأَّ  
ي َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّى ع َّسْلِهِ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Al-Baqir {a.s} and Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} were asked:  
"We buy clothes that may have come into contact with wine or the fat of a pig during 
their tailoring. Should we pray in them before washing them?"  
They replied: "Yes, there is no harm. Allah {SWT} has only forbidden eating and drinking 
it (pork and wine) but has not forbidden wearing it, touching it, or praying in it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 248 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 220 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 751
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٧٥١

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
753  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ :   عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّح ْن ِث ُّ ق ِى  ي َّوْب ٍ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ مَّعَّهُّ ع َّي ْزُّةُّ وَّ لأَّ  
ي َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّى ع َّسْلِهِ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Halabi asked Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a man who 
has only one garment, and it has urine on it, but he is unable to wash it.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should pray in it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 752

reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
He was asked by Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi 'Abdillah about a man who becomes 
ritually impure (junub) in a garment and has no other garment and is unable to wash it.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should pray in it." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 248 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 224 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 169 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 58 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 440 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 485 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 753
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٧٥٣

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
755 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ إ َّج َّاةُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ عُّزْي َّان ٍ وَّ جَّص َّزَّب ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ ق َّا َّصَّاب َّ ي َّوْن اً ن ِصْق ُّهُّ ذَّم  
إ َّوْ كُّل ُّهُّ ذَّم  ي ُّصَّلِّى  ق ِي هِ إ َّوْ ي ُّصَّلِّى  عُّزْي َّان اً ق َّال وَّج َّذَّ مَّاءً ع َّسَّلَّهُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّح ِذْ مَّاءً صَّل َّى ق ِي هِ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّصَّلِّ عُّزْي َّان اً .
hadiths.en:
In another narration, Imam {a.s} said:  
"He should pray in it, and when he finds water, he should wash it and repeat the 
prayer." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 754

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
756  -  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّحْي َّى إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ي َّسْا َّلُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ مَّعَّهُّ ي َّوْن َّان ِ ق َّا َّصَّاب َّ إ َّجَّذَّهُّمَّا ن َّوْل  
وَّ لَّمْ ي َّذْرِ إ َّي  ُّهُّمَّا هُّوَّ وَّ جَّص َّزَّب ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ وَّ ج َّاف َّ ق َّوْي َّهَّا وَّ لَّي ْسَّ عِن ْذَّةُّ مَّاء  كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ق َّال لِّى  ق ِي هِمَّا ج َّمِي عاً .    
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب   رجمه إلله: ي عن ى  على إلأن ق زإذ .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} about a man 
who is without clothing and the time for prayer arrives, and he finds a garment that is 
half covered in blood or entirely covered in blood. Should he pray in it, or should he 
pray without clothing?  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he finds water, he should wash it; if he does not find water, he should 
pray in it and should not pray without clothing”. 
[REFERENCES] Masa’il Ali ibn Ja’far, Part 1, p. 219 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 248 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 755

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
757  -  وَّق َّال مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ لِأ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّ :  إَّلذ َّمُّ ي َّكُّون ُّ ق ِى  إَّلي  َّوْب ِ عَّلَّى  َّ وَّ إ َّن َّا ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّق َّال 
ي َّوْب   ع َّي ْزُّةُّ ق َّاطْزَّجْهُّ وَّ صَّلِّ ق ِى  ع َّي ْزِةِ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ عَّلَّي ْك َّ ي َّوْب   ع َّي ْزُّةُّ ق َّامْض ِ ق ِى  صَّلاَّي ِك َّ وَّ لأَّ إ ِ عَّاذَّة َّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ مَّا لَّمْ 
ي َّ ز ِذْ عَّلَّى مِق ْذَّإرِ ذِرْهَّمٍ ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ إ َّق َّل َّ مِن ْ ذِرْهَّمٍ ق َّلَّي ْسَّ ن ِس َّى ْءٍ رَّإ َّي ْي َّهُّ إ َّوْ لَّمْ ي َّزَّةُّ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ كُّن ْ ث َّ ق َّذْ رَّإ َّي ْي َّهُّ وَّ هُّوَّ إ َّكْي َّزُّ مِن ْ  
مِق ْذَّإرِ إَّلذِّرْهَّمِ ق َّص َّي  َّعْث َّ ع َّسْلَّهُّ وَّ صَّل َّي ْث َّ ق ِي هِ صَّلَّوَّإب ُّ كَّي ِي زَّة ً ق َّا َّعِذْ  مَّا صَّل َّي ْث َّ ق ِي هِ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ ذ َّلِك َّ ن ِمَّن ْز ِلَّه ِ إَّلْمَّن ِى ِّ وَّ إَّلْن َّوْلِ 
ي ُّم َّ ذ َّكَّزَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلْمَّن ِى  َّ ق َّس َّذ َّذَّ ق ِي هِ وَّ ج َّعَّلَّهُّ إ َّس َّذ َّ مِن َّ إَّلْن َّوْلِ ي ُّم َّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّ لاَّم
ق َّعَّلَّي ْك َّ إَّلْأ ِعَّاذَّة ُّ إ ِعَّاذَّة ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ إ َّن ْث َّ ن َّط َّزْب َّ ق ِى  ي َّوْن ِك َّ ق َّلَّمْ ي ُّصِن ْهُّ وَّ صَّل َّي ْث َّ ق ِي هِ ق َّلاَّ إ ِعَّاذَّة َّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ وَّ كَّذ َّإ إَّلْن َّوْلُّ .
hadiths.en:
Safwan ibn Yahya wrote to Abu Al -Hasan, Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} 
asking about a man who has two garments, one of which has been contaminated with 
urine, but he does not know which one. When the time for prayer arrives and he fears 
missing it, and he has no water available, what should he do?  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should pray in both of them." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book (may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him) clarified that this means 
praying in each of them separately.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 249 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 225 • Awaali al -
La’aali, Part 3, p. 55 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 442 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 505 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 756

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
758   -  وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ  صَّلَّوَّإب ُّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ :   «
ن ِمَّن ْز ِلَّه ِ إَّلزِّذَّإءِ .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim said to Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} about the 
presence of blood on a garment while in prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If you see it and have another garment with you, remove it and pray in 
the other garment. If you do not have another garment, continue your prayer, and you 
do not have to repeat it, provided that the blood does not exceed the size of a dirham.  
If it is less than a dirham, it is of no consequence whether you see it or not. But if you 
saw it and it was more than a dirham's size and neglected to wash it, and you prayed 
multiple prayers in that state, then repeat all prayers you performed in that garment.  
However, this is not the same ruling as for semen or urine."  
Imam {a.s} then emphasized that the impurity of semen is more severe than urine and 
said: "If you see semen before or after, you must repeat the prayer. If you inspected 
your garment and did not find it and prayed, there is no need for repetition, and the 
same applies for urine”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 757

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"A sword is like a garment; you may pray with it as long as you do not see any blood on 
it. A bow is also like a garment."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 249 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 371 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Part 3, p. 529 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 458 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 189 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 261 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 758

reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
"It is not permissible for a man to pray while there is a sword in front of him, 
because the Qibla is a place of security."  
This was narrated from The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} about 
whether it is permissible for a man to pray while there is a rack with clothes hanging 
on it in front of him.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm." 
[REFERENCES] Masa’il Ali ibn Ja’far, Part 1, p. 222 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 250 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 760

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
He (Ali ibn Ja'far) asked him (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s}) about a man 
praying while there is garlic or onion in front of him.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾
